tests.final_answer:
[答案] The policy excludes pre-existing medical conditions, which are conditions that were present before the insurance policy was issued. This includes conditions such as:
- Congenital defects
- Preterm or postterm birth
- Existing conditions at the time of application
- Diseases that were not previously known, such as certain cancers or heart conditions
- Conditions that may have been present but not diagnosed at the time of application
- Conditions that may have been diagnosed after the application but before the policy was issued
- Conditions that may have been diagnosed and treated before the application, but not disclosed at the time
- Conditions that may have been present and developing at the time of application, but not yet diagnosed
- Conditions that may have been present and developing at the time of application, but not yet diagnosed or known to the applicant
- Conditions that may have been present and developing at the time of application, but not yet diagnosed or known to the applicant, or that may have been previously treated but not disclosed
- Conditions that may have been present and developing at the time of application, but not yet diagnosed or known to the applicant, or that may have been previously treated but not disclosed, or that may have been diagnosed and treated after the application but before the policy was issued
- Conditions that may have been present and developing at the time of application, but not yet diagnosed or known to the applicant, or that may have been previously treated but not disclosed, or that may have been diagnosed and treated after the application but before the policy was issued, or that may have been diagnosed and treated after the application but before the policy was issued, or that may have been diagnosed and treated after the application but before the policy was issued,
